# Polaris Kernel

> High-fidelity mathematical knowledge infrastructure for humans and AI agents.

## Core Purpose
Polaris Kernel is a structured repository of mathematics based on the Bourbaki pillars. It aims to provide an atomic, semantic, and illustrated knowledge base optimized for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and autonomous software engineering.

## Architecture
- Root: `src/` (6 Pillars of Mathematics)
- Adjacency Rule: Every `.md` has a `.json` metadata counterpart in the same folder.
- Governance: Validated via JSON Schemas in `metadata/schemas/`.
- Automation: Orchestrated by `scripts/build.py`.

## Key Files for AI Agents
- `AGENTS.md`: Global standards and naming conventions.
- `ARQUITECTURE.md`: Deep technical design and segmentation rules.
- `GEMINI.md`: Specific behavioral guidelines for LLMs.
- `GENERATED_ASSETS.md`: Registry of all SVG graphics.

## Technology Stack
- Documentation: Markdown (UTF-8, 80 chars/line).
- Math: LaTeX (MathJax 3).
- Graphics: Python/Matplotlib (SVG).
- Quality: Ruff, Mypy, Pytest, Jsonschema.

## Quick Navigation
- Fundamentals: `src/01_fundamentos_logica/`
- Algebra: `src/02_estructuras_algebraicas/`
- Analysis: `src/03_analisis_continuidad/`
- Geometry: `src/04_espacio_forma/`
- Discrete Math: `src/05_discrecion_computacion/`
- Stochastic: `src/06_estocastica_incertidumbre/`

---
Polaris Kernel: Structuring the beauty of mathematics.
